notes: Apple Silicon is the required macOS release lane.
Intel macOS is auto-detected and supported by the installer but is not a required release gate.
Windows 11 and Windows Terminal are the supported baseline.
Node.js, Git, GitHub CLI, VS Code, and Firebase CLI use native ARM64 paths when the vendor package provides one.
Any x64-only package must display an emulation warning and pass a read-only verification gate.
Ubuntu 24.04 x64 is a required release gate.
Ubuntu 24.04 ARM64 is required in CI; its GitHub-hosted runner class is in public preview.
Run the Linux bootstrap inside WSL2. VS Code and Chrome are installed on the Windows host through WinGet when interop is available.
Windows-on-ARM WSL2 uses native Linux ARM64 Node and Google Cloud archives plus Windows-host VS Code integration.
Debian 12+ uses the apt bootstrap path but is not a required release gate.
Debian 12+ ARM64 uses the apt bootstrap path but is not a required release gate.
Generic x64 Linux supports the shared CLI; automatic vendor installation requires apt or dnf and may need manual completion.
Generic ARM64 Linux supports the shared CLI; desktop/browser packages vary by distribution.
